name: GitHub MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: GitHub's official MCP server — gives AI agents direct access to GitHub repositories, issues, pull requests, code search, and Actions via the Model Context Protocol.
use_cases: Agents reading and writing code in GitHub repositories
Automating code review — agent reads PR diff and adds inline comments
Creating and managing GitHub Issues and Pull Requests from agent workflows
Searching code across repositories using GitHub's code search
Triggering and monitoring GitHub Actions workflows from agents
not_for: GitLab or Bitbucket repositories (GitHub-specific MCP server)
Bulk repository operations requiring raw Git protocol access
Teams not using GitHub as their primary VCS
best_when: Your agent needs to interact with GitHub repositories — read code, create PRs, manage issues — without building custom GitHub API integration.
avoid_when: You're on GitLab or Bitbucket, or need Git operations beyond what the GitHub REST API supports.

auth.notes: GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) or GitHub App installation token. Fine-grained PATs recommended for least-privilege. Scopes map to GitHub API permissions (repo, issues, pull_requests, actions, etc.).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free to use — requires a GitHub account (free tier available)
pricing.paid_tiers: GitHub API rate limits: 5,000 req/hour authenticated; GitHub Enterprise for higher limits
pricing.notes: The MCP server itself is MIT licensed and free. GitHub API rate limits apply based on your GitHub plan.

performance.rate_limits: 5,000 GitHub API requests/hour per authenticated user; search API: 30 requests/minute

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GitHub API errors are well-structured with status codes and message bodies. MCP server surfaces GitHub API errors clearly. Rate limit headers included in responses.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations (get file, list issues) are idempotent. Write operations (create issue, create PR) are not idempotent — agents must check for existing resources before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Fine-grained PATs must specify repository access explicitly — easy to miss repo permissions
GitHub search API has a separate 30 req/minute rate limit from the general 5K/hour
Creating duplicate PRs or issues if agents retry without checking for existing ones
Repository contents are base64-encoded — agents must decode file content after reading
GitHub Actions secrets cannot be read via API (only set) — don't try to retrieve secrets

security_score_components.security_notes: Fine-grained PATs with repository and permission scoping. GitHub App installations provide org-level permission management. MIT licensed, official GitHub-maintained. TLS enforced for all API and MCP transport. Token never committed — environment variable only.
